reports.daAllocationMethod: n/a — bank holding co; segment D&A absorbed in directly attributable expenses

corpBorrowings.rationale: 2026 converts ($500M, OOTM @ $22.41 conv vs $15.53 stock) settle in cash. 2029 converts ($862M, deeply ITM @ $9.45 conv) already in 1,378M diluted share count via if-converted method.

corpOverhead.rationale: Bear (Option A): cost discipline, OH flat at FY25 baseline. Base: rev × 20% leverage ratio. Bull: rev × 15% leverage ratio (mgmt guide trajectory).

capitalStructureFlags: RED: Diluted share count +16% YoY (heavy dilution)
AMBER: 2026 converts $500M maturity Q3 2026 (pre-funded by Q4 25 $1.5B raise)
GREEN: CET1 21.1% — well above 10% minimum, significant excess capital
External: Active Muddy Waters short report alleging accounting issues; not modeled
reports.notes: FINAL build. Bank holdco SOTP; contribution profit = EBITDA-equivalent. Corp borrowings reduced to $500M (only OOTM 2026 converts in cash; ITM 2029 converts ride in dilution). Corp OH calibrated to revenue × leverage ratio anchored to mgmt's FY26 EBITDA guide. Bear uses Option A (cost discipline, $1.0B flat OH).

userOverrides: AWS Bull growth: 32% (override from 28% observed max) — chip thesis: $20B run-rate + 100%+ growth + OpenAI/Anthropic/Meta/Uber forward bookings
capitalStructureFlags: Liquidity tight: $200B 2026 capex compressed TTM FCF to $1.2B from $25.9B
Coverage = (OCF + Cash) / Obligations = 1.32x — sufficient but slim

fcfRealismCheck.interpretation: DCF 13% below SOTP base — SOTP requires faster capex normalization or sustained terminal multiple to hold

verdictCaveats: Capital structure AMBER: capex consumes FCF
FCF realism NEUTRAL: SOTP base 13% above DCF — requires capex normalization or multiple sustainment
reports.notes: SOTP base case $327.62 (+21.2%) sits between DCF $284.70 (+5.3%) and bull $520.65 (+92.7%). All scenarios positive vs. current $270.25. Two caveats temper the thesis but don't invalidate it: cap structure (AMBER) and FCF realism (NEUTRAL). The 13% gap between SOTP base and DCF is informative — the SOTP multiple-based approach implicitly assumes capex normalization that the DCF makes explicit. Chip business thesis captured via AWS Bull growth override (28% → 32%); not separately reported as segment.

reports.daAllocationRationale: AWS captures ~80% of consolidated capex vs 18% revenue share (4.4x skew). Method A revenue-share would have understated AWS EBITDA by ~$10B.
capitalStructureFlags: Liquidity tight: $200B 2026 capex compressed TTM FCF to $1.2B from $25.9B
Coverage = (OCF + Cash) / Obligations = 1.32x — sufficient but slim
Q1 2026 long-term debt issuance of $53B funds AI infrastructure capex cycle
reports.notes: Primary methodology: 2Y EBITDA discounted (Method A). Base case +21.2% / prob-weighted +27.8% upside. NTM × multiple sanity check confirms thesis (+24.6% / +30.7%). Bull cases ~identical between methods because AWS 28% growth offsets 10% WACC discount. AMZN value increasingly concentrated in AWS where growth comfortably outpaces cost of capital. Capital structure AMBER caveat: aggressive 2026 AI capex consuming FCF — bull case contingent on capex ROI materializing. International and AWS multiple ranges user-overridden. 6 quarters of segment data used (vs. skill standard 8) — most recent 8-K supplemental did not include older periods. 10-Q expected mid-May 2026 will provide segment D&A footnote and full debt maturity schedule.

reports.primaryTension: Credit & Vol unanimous Risk-On; Rates group internally split between Risk-On (MOVE, DXY, USDJPY) and early Reflation signature (bull-steepening, dovish front-end)

indicators.name: HYG
indicators.reading: 80.65 (+0.37%). V-shape recovery from 78 low; reclaimed 80/80.50 pivots; hugging 200 SMA from above; above 21 EMA and 50 SMA. Target 81.00–81.50.

indicators.name: LQD
indicators.reading: 110.04 (+0.56%). Reclaimed 110; above 50 SMA and 200 SMA. IG credit firming with yields pulling back — duration relief + spread relief.

indicators.name: HYG/LQD
indicators.reading: 0.733. Both legs recovering together; HY modestly outperforming off the low. Pure risk-appetite signal.

indicators.name: US02Y
indicators.reading: 3.706% (−1.96%). Pulled back from 3.80%; dovish front-end repricing under 3.75% ceiling. No recession-grade collapse.

indicators.name: US10Y
indicators.reading: 4.248% (−1.55%). Off recent 4.40% highs; still elevated but no breakout. Growth expectations firm, term premium not expanding.

indicators.name: US30Y
indicators.reading: 4.885% (−1.01%). Sticky near 5% but not breaking higher; residual inflation concern without fresh stagflation signal.

indicators.name: 2s10s
indicators.reading: +54 bps positive slope; front-end falling roughly equal with long end today but multi-week structure is bull-steepener. Classic Reflation signature forming.

indicators.name: MOVE
indicators.reading: 65.69 (−0.30%). Deep compression from the 118 spike; well below 70 green support and far below 100 stability threshold. Rates-vol fully supportive of risk.

indicators.name: DXY
indicators.reading: 98.22 (+0.05%). Grinding under the 100 yellow; no safe-haven bid. Soft-dollar tailwind for risk assets globally.

indicators.name: USDJPY
indicators.reading: 158.59 (−0.33%). Pinned under 160 red; carry intact, no unwind pressure. 155 is the next structural shelf.

indicators.name: JP10Y
indicators.reading: 2.416% (+0.33%). Elevated, hovering under 2.50%; stable — no spike that would force global re-pricing.

indicators.name: VIX
indicators.reading: 17.47 (−2.67%). Collapsed from the 33 spike; back below 18 into 16–20 corridor. Classic post-stress washout profile.

indicators.name: VVIX
indicators.reading: 95.13 (−1.73%). Pierced the 95 complacency threshold from above; no hedging rebuild yet. Vol-of-vol supports continued compression.

indicators.name: SPY IV
indicators.reading: SPY 710.75 (+1.30%). Term structure 10.3%→13.7%→16.2%→17.5% (2D→27D). Upward-sloping contango, no backwardation, no event kink. Stability priced in.
